item.title: Seoul Confirms Strike on Vessel in Hormuz Before Pentagon Talks
item.description: South Korea said a fire aboard a Korean-operated cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz was caused by external strikes, the first official acknowledgment by Seoul that the incident was the result of an attack, though it stopped short of assigning blame.

item.title: Nintendo Shares Tumble After Lackluster Switch 2 Games Outlook
item.description: Nintendo Co.’s shares plunged the most in three months after the company forecast hardware and software sales declines, a sign that the Switch 2 has yet to create a self-sustaining demand cycle.

item.title: Philippine Peso Falling Trajectory Defies Rate Hike Expectations
item.description: The Philippine peso is likely to sink to new lows against the dollar as the country’s outsized vulnerability to high energy costs offsets expected interest rate hikes, analysts said.

item.title: Hong Kong’s Bad-Debt Bankers Ramp Up Fire Sales, Liquidations
item.description: A small group of bankers in Hong Kong tasked with cleaning up the city’s unprecedented pile of bad debt is done playing nice.

item.title: Ooh!Media Gets Rival $554 Million Cash Bid from I Squared
item.description: I Squared Capital has lobbed a rival all-cash offer for Australian outdoor advertising company oOh!media, valuing the company at A$765.9 million ($554 million).

item.title: British Businesses Turning to Temporary Staff to Cope With Volatility
item.description: British employers are relying more heavily on temporary workers, a closely watched survey shows, as they adapt to economic turbulence from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Ex-Citadel Securities Quant Triples Assets at China Hedge Fund
item.description: A former Asia quant research chief at Citadel Securities LLC has more than tripled assets at his China hedge fund in recent months, as the firm boosted fundraising after building up a strong track record.

item.title: Gold Falls as Trump Rejection of Iran Offer Fans Inflation Fears
item.description: Gold fell as President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s latest peace offer to end the 10-week conflict choking the Strait of Hormuz, fanning inflation fears as weekend clashes in the Middle East threatened a fragile ceasefire.

item.title: CSL Cuts Profit Outlook, Flags $5 Billion in New Impairments
item.description: CSL Ltd. cut its full-year outlook and flagged about $5 billion in additional impairments, saying a turnaround will take longer than expected after a review by its interim chief executive.

item.title: Cerebras to Boost Price of IPO to as High as $160, Reuters Says
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. is considering raising its initial public offering price range to between $150 and $160 per share, up from $115 to $125 per share previously, Reuters reported on Sunday.

item.title: US Stock Futures Slip as US-Iran Deal Hope Fades: Markets Wrap
item.description: US equity futures declined while the dollar rose against most major peers in early trading after President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s response to his latest proposal to end the war, likely prolonging the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil Jumps After Trump Says Iran’s Peace Offer ‘Unacceptable’
item.description: Oil surged after US President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s latest response to his proposal to end the war in the Middle East, prolonging the effective closure of the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: UK, France to Host Multinational Meeting on Mission to Escort Ships Through Hormuz
item.description: More than 40 nations will meet Monday to outline their military contributions to a European-led mission to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz once there’s a stable ceasefire.

item.title: Jerome Powell Exits With Legacy as the Fed Chair Who Fought Back
item.description: Jerome Powell’s reputation as a champion for central bank independence was secured when he publicly challenged Trump’s Justice Department probe.

item.title: Trump Says Iran Peace Proposal Response ‘Totally Unacceptable’
item.description: “I have just read the response from Iran’s so-called ‘Representatives.’ I don’t like it — TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE,” President Trump says in a Truth Social post.

item.title: Taking Stock of Jerome Powell’s Fed Chair Term
item.description: In Powell’s last week as Federal Reserve Chair we consider his legacy.

item.title: Inside a Year of Chaos and Conflict at Kevin Hart’s Media Company
item.description: A new deal with Authentic Brands is seen by some employees as the beginning of the end

item.title: Momentum-Obsessed Traders Seek Clues on Iran Truce
item.description: Investors riding a scorching run of market momentum are likely to face a reality check when trading resumes Sunday night New York time after President Donald Trump rejected the latest peace offering.

item.title: Bond Traders Brace for Inflation Data as Fed’s Powell Era Ends
item.description: Investors are awaiting a key inflation report to gauge how long the Federal Reserve — under Kevin Warsh — can afford to hold interest rates steady as Middle East turmoil keeps oil prices elevated.

item.title: Pimco CIO Sees Risk of Fed Hiking Rates Due to Iran War, FT Says
item.description: The war in Iran may lead the Federal Reserve to further delay interest-rate cuts and instead raise rates, Pimco Chief Investment Officer Dan Ivascyn told the Financial Times.

item.title: Apple to Make Design Changes in macOS 27 to Address Tahoe Quirks
item.description: Also: The latest on iOS 27 and the truth about the Vision Pro.

item.title: Microsoft’s African Data Center Falters on Payment Demands
item.description: A major Microsoft Corp. data center site in East Africa has been delayed by disagreements with the Kenyan government over the company’s request for guaranteed payments, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: AI Wins Have Alphabet Poised to Become World’s Biggest Company
item.description: Over the past year, Alphabet Inc. has gone from an artificial intelligence afterthought to the one firm in the market with dominant positions in nearly every aspect of the technology. Now it’s on the brink of overtaking AI chip giant Nvidia Corp. as the largest company in the world.

item.title: Hantavirus-Hit Cruise Ship Begins Evacuation in Canary Islands
item.description: A cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak is starting the process of evacuating passengers in Spain’s Canary Islands, almost a month after the contagious disease broke out.

item.title: Armani May Split Stake For Sale to Follow Will, Repubblica Says
item.description: The Armani Group may split the 15% stake that is up for sale equally among its preferred buyers, as the company moves closer to implementing the wishes of founder Giorgio Armani, La Repubblica reported on Sunday.

item.title: US Awaits Iran Reply as Aramco Says Hormuz Opening No Quick Fix
item.description: The wait stretched on for Iran’s response to a US proposal to end 10 weeks of war, as the world’s largest oil company warned it would take months for the market to return to normal even if the Strait of Hormuz reopened immediately.

item.title: Job Market for Recruiters Shows UK Weakness, US Strength
item.description: As recruitment firms shed large parts of their workforce across the globe, growing demand for recruiters in the US points to a stronger-than-expected job market.

item.title: Saudi Aramco Profit Jumps 26% as Iran War Drives Oil Prices
item.description: Saudi Aramco reported a 26% jump in first-quarter profit following a war-induced rise in prices of oil and refined fuels, and as it redirected exports via a pipeline bypassing the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Qatar Sends First LNG Shipment Through Hormuz Since War Started
item.description: A tanker carrying liquefied natural gas from Qatar appears to have transited the Strait of Hormuz, marking the country’s first export out of the region since the Iran war began.

item.title: Anwar to Outline Malaysia Oil Supply Continuity Plan: Bernama
item.description: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim will unveil a plan soon to shore up the country’s oil supply as uncertainties stemming from the conflict in Iran continue, Bernama reported.
